title: Updated QuickStart Accelerator Slides
description: QuickStart / QSA partner delivery model — process flow, team slide, pricing, next steps

slides.title: What is a QuickStart (QSA)?
left_bullets: A 2-week series of interactive workshops led by a Snowflake Solutions Architect
Designed to begin implementing a customer's primary use case on Snowflake
Build customer confidence through hands-on implementation
Sessions include delivery of implementation scripts from decisions made during QuickStart
right_bullets: Customer brings their first use case and our SA will advise through the end-to-end process
Secure — Define naming conventions and implement baseline RBAC
Ingest — Ingest initial data during the workshop and enable customer
Transform — Advise on transformation best practices for analytics
Analyze — Enable data visualization in Snowsight

slides.title: How QuickStart Engagements Work
bullets: QuickStart is delivered through interactive workshops led by a Snowflake Solutions Architect.
Sessions combine brief presentations and live demos with hands-on working time.
The SA guides key decisions, shares sample scripts, and assists the customer in implementing those scripts in their Snowflake environment.

SCOPE: Review relevant Snowflake capabilities → Align on primary use case → Review implementation standards → Review scripting/coding standards → Assist with customizing, deploying, and validating scripts → Provide clear next steps toward production.
Identifying a primary use case and completing Fundamentals or Data Warehousing Workshop training prior to QuickStart is highly recommended.

cards.title: Pricing & Resourcing
cards.body: Fixed fee engagement, priced at $14,000 (50% discount for new customers). Single SA assigned throughout. Contracting via Snowflake Order Form. Available on AWS Marketplace.

cards.title: Customer Requirements
cards.body: Customer has data available and a small, identified use case. Team of data analyst(s) and data engineer(s) available. Must complete Fundamentals or Data Warehousing Workshop training before scheduling.
